He is part of the long line from [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] to [[Ōkuninushi]]. He has other names in the Nihongi. These include Suga no yuyamanushi mina samoruhiko yashimajino and Suga no yuina saka karuhiko yashimade no mikoto. == Origin and Lineage == He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. He appears in the [[Kojiki]], an old Japanese text. He is the first in a line of seventeen generations from Susanoo. The [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]] has a different version of his lineage. It places [[Ōkuninushi]] as his descendant in the fifth generation. The Kojiki says Ōkuninushi is in the sixth generation. Yashimajinumi married Ōyamatsumi's daughter, [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. This marriage links him to Ōkuninushi. == Significance == Yashimajinumi's role is important in Japanese mythology. He connects various deities in these stories. His family ties show the relationships among the gods in ancient texts.. ==Shrines== He is worshipped at [[Yasaka Shrine]].{{Cite web |date=21 April 2005 |title=Mikogami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930212535/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-date=2007-09-30 |access-date=29 September 2019 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} [[Suga Shrine]], claims to stand on the site of the palace Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] built after defeating the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]], enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Kushinadahime]], and their son Yashimajinumi.{{cite web |title=第十六番 須我神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/sugajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}} ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] ==References == {{reflist}} ]]]] {{Gion cult}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] }} [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[ja:八島士奴美神]] [[zh:八島士奴美神]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
[[en:Yashimajinumi]][[ja:八島士奴美神]][[zh:八島士奴美神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55522907]] Wikidata name: Yashimajinumi no kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese deity, son of Susanoo}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | cult_centre = {{ill|Awata Shrine|ja|粟田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata Shrine|qq=}}
[[Yasaka Shrine]]
{{ill|Katano Shrine|ja|片埜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katano Shrine|qq=}}
[[:en:Suga Shrine|Suga-jinja]]
{{ill|Hyōzu Shrine|ja|兵主神社 (西脇市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōzu Shrine|qq=}} | father = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | mother = [[Kushinadahime]] | children = {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}} | spouse = [[Konohanachiru-hime]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | parents ={{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469}}| consort = {{ill|Konohanachiruhime|WD=Q48745675}} | offspring = {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|WD=Q65266248}} | member_of = {{ill|17 spirits of the world|WD=Q65249569}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} }} '''Yashimajinumi''' (八島士奴美神) was a Japanese god.{{cite web |title=Yashimajinumi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yashimajinumi/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} His name "Mighty Master Ruling Eightfold Isles" implies he ruled over all of Japan.{{rp|227}} He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]].{{Cite web |date=2007-09-30 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181 |access-date=2023-11-13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930182726/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181 |archive-date=2007-09-30 }}{{Cite web |last=Mori |first=Mizue |title=Yashimajinumi |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9724 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto}} and father of {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}{{cite book |last=Herbert |first=J. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402 |title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan |publisher=Taylor & Francis |year=2010 |isbn=978-1-136-90376-2 |series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan |page=402 |access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} and husband of [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. He is part of the long line from [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] to [[Ōkuninushi]]. He has other names in the Nihongi. These include Suga no yuyamanushi mina samoruhiko yashimajino and Suga no yuina saka karuhiko yashimade no mikoto. == Origin and Lineage == He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. He appears in the [[Kojiki]], an old Japanese text. He is the first in a line of seventeen generations from Susanoo. The [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]] has a different version of his lineage. It places [[Ōkuninushi]] as his descendant in the fifth generation. The Kojiki says Ōkuninushi is in the sixth generation. Yashimajinumi married Ōyamatsumi's daughter, [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. This marriage links him to Ōkuninushi. == Significance == Yashimajinumi's role is important in Japanese mythology. He connects various deities in these stories. His family ties show the relationships among the gods in ancient texts.. ==Shrines== He is worshipped at [[Yasaka Shrine]].{{Cite web |date=21 April 2005 |title=Mikogami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930212535/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-date=2007-09-30 |access-date=29 September 2019 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} [[Suga Shrine]], claims to stand on the site of the palace Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] built after defeating the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]], enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Kushinadahime]], and their son Yashimajinumi.{{cite web |title=第十六番 須我神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/sugajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}} ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] ==References == {{reflist}} ]]]] {{Gion cult}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] }} [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[ja:八島士奴美神]] [[zh:八島士奴美神]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

His family ties show the relationships among the gods in ancient texts.. ==Shrines== He is worshipped at [[Yasaka Shrine]].{{Cite web |date=21 April 2005 |title=Mikogami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930212535/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-date=2007-09-30 |access-date=29 September 2019 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} [[Suga Shrine]], claims to stand on the site of the palace Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] built after defeating the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]], enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Kushinadahime]], and their son Yashimajinumi.{{cite web |title=第十六番 須我神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/sugajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}} ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] ==References == {{reflist}} ]]]] {{Gion cult}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] }} [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[ja:八島士奴美神]] [[zh:八島士奴美神]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}}

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} His name "Mighty Master Ruling Eightfold Isles" implies he ruled over all of Japan.{{rp|227}} He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]].{{Cite web |date=2007-09-30 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181 |access-date=2023-11-13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930182726/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181 |archive-date=2007-09-30 }}{{Cite web |last=Mori |first=Mizue |title=Yashimajinumi |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9724 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto}} and father of {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}{{cite book |last=Herbert |first=J. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402 |title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan |publisher=Taylor & Francis |year=2010 |isbn=978-1-136-90376-2 |series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan |page=402 |access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} and husband of [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. He is part of the long line from [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] to [[Ōkuninushi]]. He has other names in the Nihongi. These include Suga no yuyamanushi mina samoruhiko yashimajino and Suga no yuina saka karuhiko yashimade no mikoto. == Origin and Lineage == He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. He appears in the [[Kojiki]], an old Japanese text. He is the first in a line of seventeen generations from Susanoo. The [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]] has a different version of his lineage. It places [[Ōkuninushi]] as his descendant in the fifth generation. The Kojiki says Ōkuninushi is in the sixth generation. Yashimajinumi married Ōyamatsumi's daughter, [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. This marriage links him to Ōkuninushi. == Significance == Yashimajinumi's role is important in Japanese mythology. He connects various deities in these stories. His family ties show the relationships among the gods in ancient texts.. ==Shrines== He is worshipped at [[Yasaka Shrine]].{{Cite web |date=21 April 2005 |title=Mikogami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930212535/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-date=2007-09-30 |access-date=29 September 2019 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} [[Suga Shrine]], claims to stand on the site of the palace Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] built after defeating the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]], enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Kushinadahime]], and their son Yashimajinumi.{{cite web |title=第十六番 須我神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/sugajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}} ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] ==References == {{reflist}} ]]]] {{Gion cult}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[ja:八島士奴美神]] [[zh:八島士奴美神]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}} [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

His family ties show the relationships among the gods in ancient texts.. ==Shrines== He is worshipped at [[Yasaka Shrine]].{{Cite web |date=21 April 2005 |title=Mikogami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930212535/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-date=2007-09-30 |access-date=29 September 2019 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} [[Suga Shrine]], claims to stand on the site of the palace Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] built after defeating the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]], enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Kushinadahime]], and their son Yashimajinumi.{{cite web |title=第十六番 須我神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/sugajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}} ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] ==References == {{reflist}} ]]]] {{Gion cult}} [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[ja:八島士奴美神]] [[zh:八島士奴美神]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}} [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} His name "Mighty Master Ruling Eightfold Isles" implies he ruled over all of Japan.{{rp|227}} He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]].{{Cite web |date=2007-09-30 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181 |access-date=2023-11-13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930182726/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181 |archive-date=2007-09-30 }}{{Cite web |last=Mori |first=Mizue |title=Yashimajinumi |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9724 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto}} and father of {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}{{cite book |last=Herbert |first=J. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402 |title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan |publisher=Taylor & Francis |year=2010 |isbn=978-1-136-90376-2 |series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan |page=402 |access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} and husband of [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. He is part of the long line from [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] to [[Ōkuninushi]]. He has other names in the Nihongi. These include Suga no yuyamanushi mina samoruhiko yashimajino and Suga no yuina saka karuhiko yashimade no mikoto. == Origin and Lineage == He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. He appears in the [[Kojiki]], an old Japanese text. He is the first in a line of seventeen generations from Susanoo. The [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]] has a different version of his lineage. It places [[Ōkuninushi]] as his descendant in the fifth generation. The Kojiki says Ōkuninushi is in the sixth generation. Yashimajinumi married Ōyamatsumi's daughter, [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. This marriage links him to Ōkuninushi. == Significance == Yashimajinumi's role is important in Japanese mythology. He connects various deities in these stories. His family ties show the relationships among the gods in ancient texts.. ==Shrines== He is worshipped at [[Yasaka Shrine]].{{Cite web |date=21 April 2005 |title=Mikogami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930212535/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-date=2007-09-30 |access-date=29 September 2019 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} [[Suga Shrine]], claims to stand on the site of the palace Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] built after defeating the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|en|Yamata no Orochi|WD=Q1054437|lt=Yamata no Orochi}}, enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Kushinadahime]], and their son Yashimajinumi.{{cite web |title=第十六番 須我神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/sugajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}} ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] ==References == {{reflist}} ]]]] {{Gion cult}} [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[ja:八島士奴美神]] [[zh:八島士奴美神]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}} [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

His family ties show the relationships among the gods in ancient texts.. ==Shrines== He is worshipped at [[Yasaka Shrine]].{{Cite web |date=21 April 2005 |title=Mikogami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930212535/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-date=2007-09-30 |access-date=29 September 2019 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} [[Suga Shrine]], claims to stand on the site of the palace Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] built after defeating the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|en|Yamata no Orochi|WD=Q1054437|lt=Yamata no Orochi}}, enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Kushinadahime]], and their son Yashimajinumi.{{cite web |title=第十六番 須我神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/sugajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}} ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] ==References == {{reflist}} ]]]] {{Gion cult}} [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[ja:八島士奴美神]] [[zh:八島士奴美神]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}} [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} His name "Mighty Master Ruling Eightfold Isles" implies he ruled over all of Japan.{{rp|227}} He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]].{{Cite web |date=2007-09-30 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181 |access-date=2023-11-13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930182726/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181 |archive-date=2007-09-30 }}{{Cite web |last=Mori |first=Mizue |title=Yashimajinumi |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9724 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto}} and father of {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}{{cite book |last=Herbert |first=J. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402 |title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan |publisher=Taylor & Francis |year=2010 |isbn=978-1-136-90376-2 |series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan |page=402 |access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} and husband of [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. He is part of the long line from [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] to [[Ōkuninushi]]. He has other names in the Nihongi. These include Suga no yuyamanushi mina samoruhiko yashimajino and Suga no yuina saka karuhiko yashimade no mikoto. == Origin and Lineage == He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. He appears in the [[Kojiki]], an old Japanese text. He is the first in a line of seventeen generations from Susanoo. The [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]] has a different version of his lineage. It places [[Ōkuninushi]] as his descendant in the fifth generation. The Kojiki says Ōkuninushi is in the sixth generation. Yashimajinumi married Ōyamatsumi's daughter, [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. This marriage links him to Ōkuninushi. == Significance == Yashimajinumi's role is important in Japanese mythology. He connects various deities in these stories. His family ties show the relationships among the gods in ancient texts.. ==Shrines== He is worshipped at [[Yasaka Shrine]].{{Cite web |date=21 April 2005 |title=Mikogami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930212535/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-date=2007-09-30 |access-date=29 September 2019 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} [[Suga Shrine]], claims to stand on the site of the palace Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] built after defeating the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|en|Yamata no Orochi|WD=Q1054437|lt=Yamata no Orochi}}, enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Kushinadahime]], and their son Yashimajinumi.{{cite web |title=第十六番 須我神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/sugajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}} ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] ==References == {{reflist}} ]]]] {{Gion cult}} [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[ja:八島士奴美神]] [[zh:八島士奴美神]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}} [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} His name "Mighty Master Ruling Eightfold Isles" implies he ruled over all of Japan.{{rp|227}} He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]].{{Cite web |date=2007-09-30 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181 |access-date=2023-11-13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930182726/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181 |archive-date=2007-09-30 }}{{Cite web |last=Mori |first=Mizue |title=Yashimajinumi |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9724 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto}} and father of {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}{{cite book |last=Herbert |first=J. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402 |title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan |publisher=Taylor & Francis |year=2010 |isbn=978-1-136-90376-2 |series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan |page=402 |access-date=2020-11-21}}{{rp|278}} and husband of [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. He is part of the long line from [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] to [[Ōkuninushi]]. He has other names in the Nihongi. These include Suga no yuyamanushi mina samoruhiko yashimajino and Suga no yuina saka karuhiko yashimade no mikoto. == Origin and Lineage == He is the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]]. He appears in the [[Kojiki]], an old Japanese text. He is the first in a line of seventeen generations from Susanoo. The [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]] has a different version of his lineage. It places [[Ōkuninushi]] as his descendant in the fifth generation. The Kojiki says Ōkuninushi is in the sixth generation. Yashimajinumi married Ōyamatsumi's daughter, [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. This marriage links him to Ōkuninushi. == Significance == Yashimajinumi's role is important in Japanese mythology. He connects various deities in these stories. His family ties show the relationships among the gods in ancient texts.. ==Shrines== He is worshipped at [[Yasaka Shrine]].{{Cite web |date=21 April 2005 |title=Mikogami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930212535/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-date=2007-09-30 |access-date=29 September 2019 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} [[Suga Shrine]], claims to stand on the site of the palace Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] built after defeating the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|en|Yamata no Orochi|WD=Q1054437|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=Yamata no Orochi}}, enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Kushinadahime]], and their son Yashimajinumi.{{cite web |title=第十六番 須我神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/sugajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}} ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] ==References == {{reflist}} ]]]] {{Gion cult}} [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[ja:八島士奴美神]] [[zh:八島士奴美神]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}} [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

It places [[Ōkuninushi]] as his descendant in the fifth generation. The Kojiki says Ōkuninushi is in the sixth generation. Yashimajinumi married Ōyamatsumi's daughter, [[Konohanachiru-hime]]. This marriage links him to Ōkuninushi. == Significance == Yashimajinumi's role is important in Japanese mythology. He connects various deities in these stories. His family ties show the relationships among the gods in ancient texts.. ==Shrines== He is worshipped at [[Yasaka Shrine]].{{Cite web |date=21 April 2005 |title=Mikogami |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930212535/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=13 |archive-date=2007-09-30 |access-date=29 September 2019 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto}} [[Suga Shrine]], claims to stand on the site of the palace Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] built after defeating the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|en|Yamata no Orochi|WD=Q1054437|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=Yamata no Orochi}}, enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Kushinadahime]], and their son Yashimajinumi.{{cite web |title=第十六番 須我神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/sugajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}} ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] ==References == {{reflist}} ]]]] {{Gion cult}} [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[en:Yashimajinumi]] [[ja:八島士奴美神]] [[zh:八島士奴美神]] {{wikidata link|Q55522907}} [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
